evidence_excerpt: After diagnosis by video-EEG at 6 months, bathing was replaced with cooler showers (<34°C) without anticonvulsants. Only one additional seizure occurred at 14 months; neurologic development was normal.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Only one additional seizure at the age of 14 months after adopting cooler showers and avoiding hot baths.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Treatment consisted of showering instead of bathing and lowering water temperature to <34°C; no anticonvulsant was prescribed.
One additional seizure occurred at 14 months.

notes_on_uncertainty: Data pertain to Patient 1 from the case series on hot water epilepsy. Follow-up explicitly documented to 14 months post-diagnosis; long-term (>12 months) outcomes beyond this point are not detailed, so the overall outcome of partial recovery reflects improvement with a single mid-term recurrence.
